title: Mildly Briny Malaysian Sausage Satay
description: A briny and silky Malaysian dish featuring sausage, perfect for a solo dinner, made with halal-friendly ingredients and cooked in a fondue pot.
instructions: Fill a fondue pot with peanut oil to a depth of about 2 inches and heat it to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a bowl, whisk together coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, grated ginger, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Add the Malaysian sausage links to the fondue pot and cook for 5 minutes, or until browned and cooked through.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the sausage from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the sausage hot with the coconut milk sauce spooned over the top.
Garnish with sliced fresh cilantro, if desired.
Repeat the cooking process with any remaining sausage links.
